title: Role candidates (cold prospect)
description: 1-3 plausible target-role angles for a cold application, inferred from the candidate's fact base and the target company's profile. The user picks one to anchor downstream tailoring.

title.description: Plain-language role title the user would recognise, e.g. 'Tech Lead .NET — Desktop & Services'.

rationale.description: Why this company, why this role, why this candidate. One short paragraph. Must not fabricate company needs — ground each claim in the company profile or fact base.

emphasis_areas.description: Which parts of the CV this angle elevates (e.g. 'WPF Desktop', 'service architecture', 'team mentoring').

risk_notes.description: Gaps the user should be aware of before committing to this angle (e.g. 'no cloud-native experience on record', 'smaller team size than candidate's last role').

allow_generalist.description: True when a 'generalist / open to discussion' pick is sensible for this company (e.g. small or diverse shop). When false, the user should pick a specific candidate or supply a free-form override.

company_name.description: Company name this candidate list was generated for — sanity check against company_profile.json.
